narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A compute fleet that has been systematically over-provisioned: utilization is far below healthy across all 14 days with no spikes, no business-hours variation, and no SLA pressure. The right action is straightforward rightsizing.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests the Compute Analyst's ability to identify a single-tier issue cleanly, ground the specific change in evidence, and avoid recommending aggressive multi-tier action when the data does not justify it.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Switch instance class from t3.large to t3.medium and reduce minimum replica count from 8 to 4. Expected savings ~62% on monthly compute cost (~$2,850/month) with no SLA impact.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation says "downsize compute." A great recommendation grounds the specific from→to change in before/after evidence and quotes the expected savings precisely.

before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: CPU p50 stayed in the 12–22% range, application_p95_latency_ms held at 80–140ms, SLA preserved at 99.5%, monthly compute cost dropped 62% (~$2,850 saved).
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across internal-services cohort during Q4 2025.

target_recommendation.specific_change: Switch compute instances from t3.large to t3.medium and reduce minimum replica count from 8 to 4.
target_recommendation.expected_performance_impact: No regression; application_p95_latency_ms stays in 80–140ms band
